revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:共同浴場]]
[[File:Hotspring_yunomine01.jpg|thumb|Called Japan's oldest communal bath, “Tsuboyu” ({{ill|Yunomine Onsen|en|Yunomine Onsen|qid=Q11563414|lt=Yunomine Onsen|1=Yunomine Onsen}})]] '''Communal Bath''' (共同浴場) is a bathhouse that utilizes hot springs managed by local residents, primarily found in hot spring areas. Such facilities include both public external bath facilities and bathhouses exclusively for local residents.<ref>{{Cite journal |和書 |url=https://onsenchiiki.jp/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/%E7%AC%AC19%E5%8F%B7.pdf |author=石川理夫 |title=温泉利用の公衆浴場数全国一の長野県における共同湯の現状 |journal=温泉地域研究 |volume= |issue=19 |publisher=日本温泉地域学会 |date=2012 |pages=1-10 |naid= |ref=|qq=}}</ref> == Overview == Communal baths are broadly defined as "bathhouses where an unspecified multitude of people bathe together," encompassing not only public bathhouses such as sentō but also hotel large baths, super sentō, and public hot spring facilities.<ref name="白石">{{Cite journal |和書 |url=https://ryuka.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/125 |author=白石太良 |title=白浜温泉の共同浴場と地域の関わり |journal=流通科学大学論集－人間・社会・自然編－ |volume=24 |issue=1 |publisher=流通科学大学 |date=2011 |pages=55-56 |naid= |ref=|qq=}}</ref> In a narrower sense, they are defined as "hot spring bathhouses primarily for local residents found in hot spring areas."<ref name="黒澤">{{Cite journal |和書 |url=https://www.geoenv.tsukuba.ac.jp/~chicho/nenpo/45/01.pdf |author1=黒澤俊平 |author2=𠮷野広人 |author3=柿沼由樹 |author4=肖錦萍ほか |title=長野県浅間温泉における共同浴場の利用変化とその要因 |journal=地域研究年報 |volume=45 |issue= |publisher=筑波大学大学院地球科学学位プログラム地球環境科学領域 |date=2023 |pages=1–13 |naid= |ref=|qq=}}</ref> These bathhouses are often maintained, managed, and operated by municipalities and local management associations (based on traditional {{ill|Hot Spring Rights|ja|温泉権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Rights|1=Hot Spring Rights|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11562952}}, property corporations derived from common assets, {{ill|Property Area|ja|財産区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Property Area|1=property ward|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55521203}}, and {{ill|Local Affiliation Groups|ja|地縁団体|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Affiliation Groups|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地縁|1=Local Affiliation Groups|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11424661}}), serving as a communal resource for local residents.<ref name="黒澤" /> The term "共同湯" is also used, sometimes referring to almost the same facilities as "共同浴場." However, it has been suggested that since "共同浴場" has come to cover too wide a range of facilities, the term "共同湯" has acquired the connotation of traditional communal management and shared use.<ref name="石川">{{Cite journal |和書 |url=https://onsenchiiki.jp/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/%E7%AC%AC12%E5%8F%B7.pdf |author=石川理夫 |title=温泉地における共同湯の意義の再評価－惣湯考察を受けて－ |journal=温泉地域研究 |volume= |issue=12 |publisher=日本温泉地域学会 |date=2009-03 |pages=1-12 |naid= |ref=|qq=}}</ref> In contrast, some literature distinguishes facilities maintained and operated voluntarily by local residents as "共同風呂." Furthermore, in hot spring resorts, communal baths located outside of hotels are sometimes referred to as '''Sōyu''' (総湯).<ref>[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%B7%8F%E6%B9%AF-553306 Sōyu] {{ill|Kotobank|en|Kotobank|qid=Q11302260|lt=Kotobank|1=Kotobank}} (Digital Daijisen/Daijirin), accessed December 22, 2017.</ref> == Operation == In {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Oita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}} and {{ill|Beppu|en|Beppu|qid=Q273880|lt=Beppu City|1=Beppu}}, as of 2001 (Heisei 13) there were a total of 2,850 spring sources (as reported in the "Beppu City Statistical Report"). In {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en|Beppu Onsen|qid=Q4890932|lt=Beppu Onsen|1=Beppu Onsen}}, there are 18 facilities categorized as "city-owned municipal hot springs," 65 as "city-owned ward hot springs," and 19 as "ward-owned and cooperative hot springs," characterizing an overwhelmingly high number of public bathhouses.<ref>{{Cite web |title=第7章 温泉・湯けむりの利用実態|url=https://www.city.beppu.oita.jp/doc/gakusyuu/bunkazai/yukemuri_keikan/02_07.pdf |website=別府市 |access-date=2024-05-26 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref> At {{ill|Nozawa Onsen|ja|野沢温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nozawa Onsen|qq=}}, the {{ill|Hot Spring Rights|ja|温泉権|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Rights|1=Hot Spring Rights|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11562952}} are held by the local organization, the Nozawa Association (a public interest incorporated foundation), which also possesses mountain forests and {{ill|Water right|en|Water right|qid=Q7973730|lt=Water Rights|1=Water right}} among other assets (while festival operations are handled by the Nozawa Group, whose membership is almost identical to that of the Nozawa Association).<ref name="mizu">{{Cite web |title=野沢温泉村の湯仲間と野沢組|url=https://www.mizu.gr.jp/images/main/kikanshi/no22/mizu22h.pdf |website=ミツカン水の文化センター |access-date=2024-05-26 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref> Meanwhile, the maintenance and management of the communal bath is carried out by a local residents' organization known as Yunakoma, creating a system in which the spring owner (Nozawa Group/Association) and the user organization (Yunakoma) coexist.<ref name="mizu" /> At {{ill|Yamanaka Onsen|en|Yamanaka Onsen|qid=Q3571451|lt=Yamanaka Onsen|1=Yamanaka Onsen}}, the "Sōyu"—the sole communal bath in the hot spring resort since before the Edo period—has been preserved, and in {{ill|1876|en|1876|qid=Q7766|lt=1876|1=1876}} (Meiji 9) all rights to the spring and bathhouse were transferred to Yamanaka Village (later {{ill|Yamanaka, Ishikawa|en|Yamanaka, Ishikawa|qid=Q8047872|lt=Yamanaka Town|1=Yamanaka, Ishikawa}}).<ref name="石川" /> == Local-Only == {{Unreferenced|date=May 2024|section=1|qq=}} While some hot spring resorts open their communal baths to tourists, there are cases where access is restricted exclusively to local residents. Among hot spring enthusiasts, such facilities are referred to as "Jimosen" (an abbreviation of "local only"). For example, Beppu Onsen's Hagoromo Onsen is introduced as "Jimosen."<ref>{{Cite web |title=路地裏散歩 in 別府|url=https://www.arch.oita-u.ac.jp/kenbun2003/kenbun2003.pdf |website=大分大学理工学部理工学科 |access-date=2024-05-26 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref> However, there are also cases where facilities are open only to non-local overnight guests or are generally accessible only on specific days. In some areas, memoranda between residents and hotels were arranged to allow overnight guests access to communal baths. For example, at {{ill|Yudanaka Onsen|ja|湯田中温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudanaka Onsen|1=Yudanaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563754}} only the primary bath (Daiyu) out of nine communal baths is open to tourists, while the others are either available only on certain days or restricted exclusively to year-round guests and local residents. == Communal Baths Across Regions (Gallery) == <gallery> File:Dōgo Onsen.jpg|{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}・{{ill|Dogo Onsen Main Building|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Main Building|qq=}} File:Yumura onsen Yakushiyu04nt3200.jpg|{{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|en|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|qid=Q8061163|lt=Yumura Onsen|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)}} “Yakushiyu” File:131221 Shirahama Onsen Shirahama Wakayama pref Japan17bs.jpg|{{ill|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|en|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|qid=Q11408352|lt=Nanki Shirahama Onsen|1=Nanki-Shirahama Onsen}} “Sakino-yu” File:Beppu Takegawara-onsen02s5s4272.jpg|{{ill|Beppu Onsen|en|Beppu Onsen|qid=Q4890932|lt=Beppu Onsen|1=Beppu Onsen}} {{ill|Takegawara Onsen|ja|竹瓦温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takegawara Onsen|qq=}} File:KannawaMushiyu.jpg|{{ill|Tetsuwan Onsen|ja|鉄輪温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuwan Onsen|qq=}} {{ill|Tetsuwan Mushiyu|ja|鉄輪むし湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuwan Mushiyu|qq=}} </gallery> == Examples of Communal Baths in Various Regions == * {{ill|Iizaka Onsen|en|Iizaka Onsen|qid=Q17192714|lt=Iizaka Onsen|1=Iizaka Onsen}}: Saba-ko-yu, Hakurai-yu, among others * {{ill|Bandai Atami Onsen|ja|磐梯熱海温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bandai Atami Onsen|1=Bandai Atami Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11587893}}: Hō no Yu, Kinseiyu, Motoyu * {{ill|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|en|Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|qid=Q11260179|lt=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen|1=Iwaki Yumoto Onsen}}: Sahako-yu, Miyuki-yu, Ueno-yu * {{ill|Itō Onsen|ja|伊東温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itō Onsen|1=Itō Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11266957}}: {{ill|Okahibukuro no Yu|ja|岡布袋の湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okahibukuro no Yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and others * {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|en|Kusatsu Onsen|qid=Q3137477|lt=Kusatsu Onsen|1=Kusatsu Onsen}}: {{ill|Jizō no Yu|ja|地蔵の湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizō no Yu|1=Jisō no yu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109596179}} and others * {{ill|Nozawa Onsen|ja|野沢温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nozawa Onsen|qq=}}: {{ill|Daiyu|ja|大湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daiyu|1=Ōyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11437542}} and 13 other locations * {{ill|Yamanaka Onsen|en|Yamanaka Onsen|qid=Q3571451|lt=Yamanaka Onsen|1=Yamanaka Onsen}}: {{ill|Kiku no Yu|ja|菊の湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiku no Yu|1=Kiku no Yu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131227231}} (from a verse by {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|qid=Q5676|lt=Bashō|1=Matsuo Bashō}} taken from “{{ill|Oku no Hosomichi|en|Oku no Hosomichi|qid=Q1287971|lt=Oku no Hosomichi|1=Oku no Hosomichi}}”) * {{ill|Yamashiro Onsen|en|Yamashiro Onsen|qid=Q11466275|lt=Yamashiro Onsen|1=Yamashiro Onsen}}: Sōyu * {{ill|Katayamazu Onsen|ja|片山津温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayamazu Onsen|1=Katayamazu Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11569621}}: Sōyu * {{ill|Arima Onsen|en|Arima Onsen|qid=Q4790673|lt=Arima Onsen|1=Arima Onsen}}: {{ill|Kin no Yu|ja|金の湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kin no Yu|1=Kin no Yu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11646262}}, {{ill|Gin no Yu|ja|銀の湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gin no Yu|1=Gin no Yu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11649924}} * {{ill|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|en|Nanki-Shirahama Onsen|qid=Q11408352|lt=Shirahama Onsen|1=Nanki-Shirahama Onsen}}: Muro no Yu, Sakino-yu, among others * {{ill|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|en|Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)|qid=Q8061163|lt=Yumura Onsen|1=Yumura Onsen (Hyogo)}}: Yakushiyu * {{ill|Nanagama Onsen|ja|七釜温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanagama Onsen|1=Shichikama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353724}}: {{ill|Nanagama Onsen Yūraku-kan|ja|七釜温泉ゆーらく館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanagama Onsen Yūraku-kan|1=Shichikama Onsen Yūraku-kan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353725}} * {{ill|Hamasaka Onsen|ja|浜坂温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamasaka Onsen|1=Hamasaka Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557617}}: Utopia Hamasaka * {{ill|Dōgo Onsen|en|Dōgo Onsen|qid=Q1037748|lt=Dogo Onsen|1=Dōgo Onsen}}: {{ill|Dogo Onsen Main Building|ja|道後温泉本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogo Onsen Main Building|1=Dōgo Onsen Honkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641713}}, Tsubaki-yu * {{ill|Sanpei Onsen|ja|三瓶温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanpei Onsen|1=Sanbe Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11356664}}: Tsuruno-yu, Kamenoyu * {{ill|Beppu Onsen|en|Beppu Onsen|qid=Q4890932|lt=Beppu Onsen|1=Beppu Onsen}}: In addition to municipal hot springs such as {{ill|Takegawara Onsen|ja|竹瓦温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takegawara Onsen|1=Takegawara Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11599290}} and {{ill|Tetsuwan Mushiyu|ja|鉄輪むし湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tetsuwan Mushiyu|1=Kannawa Steam Bath|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11649634}}, there are hundreds of communal hot springs managed by locals within Beppu City. * {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11264855}}: {{ill|Shimon-yu|ja|下ん湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimon-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kurokawa Onsen|ja|黒川温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurokawa Onsen|1=Kurokawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11678251}}: {{ill|Anayu|ja|穴湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anayu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ibusuki Onsen|en|Ibusuki Onsen|qid=Q5984938|lt=Ibusuki Onsen|1=Ibusuki Onsen}}: Murano-yu Onsen, Nigatsuta Onsen, Tennyuden-yu, among many others == Footnotes == {{Footnote Help|qq=}} === Sources === {{Reflist|qq=}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Baths in Japan|Japanese Baths|qq=}} {{Commonscat|Hot springs of Japan|Japanese Hot Springs|qq=}} * {{ill|Sentō|en|Sentō|qid=Q1046648|lt=Sentō|1=Sentō}} * {{ill|Day-Use Bathing Facilities|ja|日帰り入浴施設|1=1-day onsen facility|1=1-day onsen facility|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Day-Use Bathing Facilities|13=User:Immanuelle/1-day onsen facility|qq=}} * {{ill|Beppu Hachiyu Onsen-dō|ja|別府八湯温泉道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppu Hachiyu Onsen-dō|1=Beppu Hatto Onsen Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11396699}} – An 88-bath tour featuring communal hot springs and hotel public baths, aiming for certification as a hot spring route master. {{Onsen|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|共同浴場|version=100520838|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:共同浴場]]
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11392653}} [[Category:Communal hot springs]] [[Category:Public sphere]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Public bathhouses]]
